title: Dagon Niriz
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Dagon_Niriz.jpg]] |name=Dagon Niriz |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]{{JournalCite|11|Command Decision}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=Light brown |feathers= |eyes= |skin=Fair |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Dagon Niriz's family]] *[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] **[[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] *[[Empire of the Hand]]''[[Vision of the Future]]'' |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|But now to the bridge. The Unknown Regions are calling, and we have a great deal of work yet to do.|Mitth'raw'nuruodo, to Dagon Niriz|"[[Side Trip]]"}} '''Dagon Niriz''' was a fourth-generation [[Navy/Legends|navy]] [[Officer/Legends|officer]], a [[Captain/Legends|captain]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], and the [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]] of the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Admonitor]]'' in the years leading up to the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. A stalwart supporter of order and obedience in naval service, he took his position seriously. When he was assigned to [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn's]] mapping expedition in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]], he initially saw it as a blow to his career. However, after a test of Niriz's loyalty which caused the officer to doubt Thrawn's fitness for command but during which Niriz refused to participate in mutiny, Thrawn revealed the true nature of his mission. Thrawn, with Niriz and [[Voss Parck/Legends|Voss Parck]] at his side, was in fact assigned to conquer the Unknown Regions for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and secure it against the vast array of hidden threats within. Niriz took great pride in this cause. By Thrawn design, the [[Empire of the Hand]] was secretly established to protect [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] from the many and threats residing in the Unknown Regions and continued to serve in it [[Battle of Endor/Legends|following]] [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Death/Legends|death]]. ==Biography== ===Early career=== A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] into a [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core World]] [[Dagon Niriz's family|family]], the young Dagon Niriz was put under pressure to become{{JournalCite|11|Command Decision}} {{C|Roleplaying Game Statistics}} the fourth generation of his family to enter [[Navy/Legends|naval]] service. Joining the [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Academy]], Niriz trained as a naval [[Officer/Legends|officer]] and devoted himself to succeeding in his career and living up to his lineage. Entering service in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], Niriz was posted aboard a [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] where he gained practical experience to supplement the command and tactical knowledge he had already gained from studying manuals. After only a few months as a Star Destroyer [[Captain/Legends|captain]], Niriz's diligent service was rewarded with a transfer to a military affairs bureau on the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], where he was tasked with analyzing situation reports from around the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] and making recommendations to senior officers. Niriz's assignment on Coruscant saw him caught up in [[Imperial Court/Legends|Imperial Court]] [[Politics/Legends|politics]]; when enemies of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] united against him, Thrawn was [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] on a mapping expedition to the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]], along with any other officer believed to be linked to the [[Alien/Legends|alien]] admiral, including Niriz. ===Assignment to Thrawn=== {{Quote|You've got it backwards, Captain. You're an honorable officer, from a proud Core World family. There's never been any question in Thrawn's mind that he can trust you.''"
"''You could have fooled me. If he trusts me so much, why didn't he let me in on what he was doing?''"
"''Oh, you were proving you were trustworthy, all right. But you weren't proving it to Thrawn. You were proving it to yourself.|Voss Parck and Dagon Niriz|}} Niriz was assigned as [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]] of Thrawn's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] ''Admonitor'', with Thrawn's long-time ally [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Voss Parck/Legends|Voss Parck]] as his [[Executive officer/Legends|executive officer]]. Although Niriz was pleased to be back on the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] of a Star Destroyer, he was ashamed to find his career effectively ended through his association with the scandal and considered the mission to be a waste of resources at a time when resistance to Imperial rule was growing throughout the Empire. Niriz was also initially uneasy with Thrawn, who continually countermanded his orders and assigned the captain to what he considered to be menial tasks. After several [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jumps that took the ''Admonitor'' through the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] and [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]], the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] arrived in the Unknown Regions and began [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] [[Star system/Legends|star systems]]. In the fifteenth system, the Imperials encountered several ships commanded by the [[Ebruchi]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Creysis]]. Niriz was wary of the Ebruchi and ordered a [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] to warn them back, only to have his order overruled by Thrawn. The admiral instead contacted Creysis and announced the ''Admonitor'' as an [[Alderaanian/Legends|Alderaanian]] colony ship, accepting Creysis's offer to assist in finding a suitable [[Planet/Legends|planet]] for the colonists and arranging to meet the Ebruchi aboard the ''Admonitor''. [[File:Thrawn_Meeting.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Niriz and Thrawn meet Creysis aboard the ''Admonitor''.]] Niriz and Thrawn met Creysis in the ''Admonitor''{{'s}} [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]]. Thrawn agreed to send the fictional "[[Council of Colonists]]" to Creysis's command ship to conduct negotiations. In return Creysis requested a TIE fighter in return for his help, but Thrawn instead offered a golden sculpture, which Creysis accepted. When Creysis left, Niriz cautioned against sending a delegation to the Ebruchi ship and suggested a show of strength. Thrawn, however, insisted that they should take advantage of the opportunity to learn more about the Ebruchi and instructed Niriz to select three [[TIE pilot]]s who could remain cool under fire to escort the mission. Parck and six [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]], posing as the Council of Colonists, set out in a [[Zeta-class long-range shuttle|''Zeta''-class shuttle]] for Creysis's vessel. After the [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] entered the hangar, the Ebruchi's escort [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] opened fire on its TIE escorts. Niriz immediately ordered the ''Admonitor'' to intercept, but Thrawn countermanded him, ordering a slow approach to maintain the illusion that the Star Destroyer was a colony ship, resulting in the capture of one of the TIEs. With Thrawn's orders putting men in the hands of Creysis, Niriz became more and more convinced his commander was incompetent. The commander of the ship's [[Imperial Army/Legends|infantry complement]], [[General/Legends|General]] [[Larr Haverel]], approached Niriz, suggesting that Thrawn was in league with Creysis and had to be removed from command. Though he found the implication frighteningly easy to believe, Niriz's pride in his service in the fleet and respect for authority caused him to reject Haverel's idea as mutiny. Niriz intended to inform Thrawn of the issue, but was preempted when Haverel returned with six [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] and demanded Thrawn return to his quarters and hand over command to Niriz. However, Niriz stood with his commander, telling Haverel he would find no support in Niriz or the crew. As Haverel prepared to act anyway, Thrawn received the key he had been waiting for. The admiral had allowed the TIE fighter to be captured, having already modified the fighter to disguise its capabilities. Having examined the captured fighter and determined that the Imperials were no threat, Creysis had dispatched a courier to his superiors, taking the sculpture as a sign of the ''Admonitor''{{'s}} wealth. As Niriz began to grasp the depth of Thrawn's strategy and the reason for his delays and seeming inaction, the admiral sprang into action, handily [[Skirmish between the Admonitor and Creysis's scout squadron|defeating]] Creysis and rescuing Parck's team. Following a [[Homing beacon/Legends|homing beacon]] hidden in the sculpture, Thrawn proceeded to take the ''Admonitor'' to Creysis's home base, ending the Ebruchi threat. Following the defeat of the Ebruchi, Niriz offered Thrawn his resignation as captain of the ''Admonitor'' and expected to be sent back to Coruscant with Haverel for [[Court-martial/Legends|court-martial]]. However, Parck approached Niriz and informed him that the admiral had rejected his resignation; Thrawn had been testing Niriz's loyalty and, by standing with Thrawn against Haverel, Niriz had passed. Parck revealed to him that the mission was not punishment at all, but instead a secret mission to subdue the myriad threats of the Unknown Regions and provide a foothold for future Imperial expansion. Niriz would be key to this vital mission. Infused with a sense of purpose, Niriz experienced a new commitment to Thrawn and the mission. ===Return to known space=== {{Quote|Admiral…I have to say one last time that I don't think you should do this. At least not personally.|Dagon Niriz, to Mitth'raw'nuruodo|}} [[File:Side_Trip.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Thrawn and Niriz toast a job completed, just as Darth Vader enters.]] Niriz continued to serve Thrawn loyally, completing numerous campaigns in the Unknown Regions under the Grand Admiral's leadership. In [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' Thrawn returned to the Empire proper in order to assist [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] in his efforts to undermine [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]], the [[Crime lord/Legends|head]] of the [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] crime syndicate and a personal enemy of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]]. To this end, Thrawn planned to create a distraction for Xizor by [[Capture of Zekka Thyne|removing]] Black Sun [[Vigo/Legends|Vigo]] [[Zekka Thyne]] from [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]], putting an effective end to Black Sun's operations on the planet. Niriz and the ''Admonitor'' returned with him. Since intervention by Imperial forces would implicate Vader, Thrawn sought a civilian vessel on which to travel to Corellia. Niriz played a critical role in shanghaiing the crew of the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] ''[[Hopskip]]'' into aiding Thrawn by capturing their [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] and revealing the extremely sudden imposition of a licensing requirement for their cargo—which he was prepared to waive if the crew would deliver a cargo for him. Niriz also introduced them to the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jodo Kast]], who he claimed would serve as their guide for the mission and with whom he put on a convincing argument to demonstrate their mutual disliking and hide the fact that Kast was actually Thrawn disguised in [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian armor]]. Once out of sight of the [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]], Niriz—who was unaware of Vader's involvement and the need for Thrawn to get personally involved—felt free to voice his reservations about the plan to the Grand Admiral, displaying great concern for his safety. The ''Hopskip'' departed once their cargo was unloaded and the new cargo loaded aboard. Niriz and Thrawn knew that among the ''Hopskip''{{'s}} cargo of [[Pashkin sausage]]s were numerous crates of [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] being smuggled to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. While waiting for the ''Hopskip'' to return, Niriz had the crates opened and homing beacons installed inside each of the hidden blasters to enable the Empire to locate the Rebels. When Thrawn returned after having successfully brought down Thyne, Niriz expressed his relief at the Grand Admiral's safe return and informed him that the plan to track the ''Hopskip''{{'s}} cargo was in place—a plan that would eventually lead to the Empire's critical victory at the [[Battle of Derra IV]]. When Vader arrived aboard the ''Admonitor'', Niriz was privy to conversations between Thrawn and Vader detailing their plot against Xizor. Once this high-level discussion was carried out, Vader departed and the ''Admonitor'' set course for the Unknown Regions once more, but not before Thrawn revealed his payment for the assistance: control of the [[Noghri/Legends|Noghri]] [[Commando/Legends|commandos]]. ===The Empire of the Hand=== {{Quote|Regardless, the point is that neither Parck nor Niriz—nor the ''Admonitor'', for that matter—ever returned to official duty with the Empire. Not even when Thrawn himself came back.|[[Grodin Tierce (clone)|Grodin Tierce's clone]], to [[Vilim Disra]]|}} In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Emperor Palpatine was [[Death/Legends|killed]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. While the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] battled the Empire and numerous rogue Imperials for control of the known [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' Thrawn initially remained in the Unknown Regions, where his holdings transformed into the [[Empire of the Hand]].{{StoryCite|story=Crisis of Faith|book=Heir to the Empire: The 20th Anniversary Edition}} In [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], Thrawn returned to the Empire to lead a renewed [[Thrawn campaign|push]] against the New Republic, leaving Parck in command of the Empire of the Hand. No mention of Niriz was made at any point after the Empire of the Hand's existence was revealed to the New Republic in [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]], but Niriz never returned to official duty with the Empire. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|We're warriors of the Imperial Fleet. It's our duty to die when the situation requires it.|Dagon Niriz to Larr Haverel|}} [[File:Admonitor.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The ''Admonitor'' hangs in space under Dagon Niriz's command.]] Initially pressured by his family into joining the Academy, Dagon Niriz devoted himself to his studies and allowed his passion for the Empire to motivate him to succeed in his career. Niriz quickly gained a reputation as a diligent officer, who adhered strictly to the chain of command—even when he did not care for his commanding officer—and was quick to assess a situation and apply the appropriate action as dictated by regulations. If death was part of his duty, he would accept it as necessary. Niriz took great pride in his family's tradition of naval service and was ashamed to find himself linked to Thrawn and an Imperial Court scandal. Though he initially resented his assignment to Thrawn as a career-ending exile which he did not deserve, he did not allow it to impact his performance as an officer. Thrawn's unorthodox tactics left Niriz torn between loyalty to his commander and his dedication to Imperial procedures. When forced to choose, Niriz sided with Thrawn and refused to be involved in Haverel's attempted mutiny. When he discovered that the mission held vital importance, though not public recognition, Niriz was inspired to continue and became extremely dedicated to it, showing that it was not praise and promotions which he sought, but the ability to make a difference in important work. Niriz also came to greatly admire Thrawn, whom he had initially looked down on as a fool and even an incompetent. Upon fully understanding Thrawn's tactics, he became wholly committed to his superior officer, and was extremely protective and supportive of the admiral, who he believed could teach him more than any other mentor. ==Behind the scenes== The character of Dagon Niriz was created by [[Timothy Zahn]] for the short story "[[Command Decision]]", published in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 11|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 11]] on [[November 1]], [[1996]]. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Dagon Niriz}} *"[[Side Trip]]" {{Reprint|Side Trip}} *{{JournalCite|11|Command Decision|reprint=1}} {{1st}} *''[[Vision of the Future]]'' {{Ab|Vision of the Future (unabridged audiobook)}} {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Dagon Niriz#legends |es=Dagon Niriz }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Niriz, Dagon}} [[Category:Empire of the Hand personnel]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial Navy captains of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Males]]
